note: This file is the single source of truth for the SBCS scoring instrument. The methodology page, the leaderboard and the scoring form all read it. Edit it here and everything downstream updates.

guidance.1: A misfire. We'd cross the street to avoid it.
guidance.2: It exists. You won't remember it by Tuesday.
guidance.3: The good stuff. You'd happily order it again.

groups.label: The Patty
groups.blurb: The meat itself — the part you cannot fake.

groups.label: The Build
groups.blurb: Architecture. Does it stand up to being eaten?
groups.doctrine: A burger that needs a knife has already lost.

groups.label: The Flavor
groups.blurb: Everything that lands on the tongue.
groups.doctrine: Sauce is a privilege, not a right.

groups.label: The Craft
groups.blurb: Intent and execution beyond the basics.
groups.doctrine: Anyone can grill. Few can compose.

groups.label: The Honesty
groups.blurb: What it costs and whether it kept its promises.
groups.doctrine: You're paying for the burger, not the postcode.

groups.label: The X-Factor
groups.blurb: The intangibles a scorecard can barely hold.
groups.doctrine: The bite you're still thinking about in the cab home.

doneness.label: Doneness
doneness.desc: Cooked to the temperature it promised. A medium that is actually medium.

seasoning.label: Seasoning
seasoning.desc: Salt and pepper where it counts. Seasoned by the kitchen, not by you at the table.

crust.label: Crust
crust.desc: The Maillard sear — browning, char and crisp edges on the patty.

texture.label: Texture
texture.desc: Bite and chew. Juicy and tender, not a hockey puck and not mush.

ratio.label: Ratio
ratio.desc: Meat-to-bun-to-everything balance. Nothing overpowers, nothing is missing.

bun.label: Bun
bun.desc: Structure, flavour, freshness. Toasted, soft, and still standing at the last bite.

integrity.label: Integrity
integrity.desc: Does it hold together, or collapse into a knife-and-fork situation by bite three?

distribution.label: Distribution
distribution.desc: Toppings and sauce spread evenly, not all hiding in one corner.

flavor.label: Flavor
flavor.desc: The overall taste. Does it all add up to something genuinely delicious?

sauce.label: Sauce
sauce.desc: The condiment game. Right sauce, right amount, right idea.

cheese.label: Cheese
cheese.desc: Melt, coverage, choice. Present and accounted for (if it claims to be there).

toppings.label: Toppings
toppings.desc: Freshness and contribution. Earning a place, not just filling space.

craveability.label: Craveability
craveability.desc: Will you think about this burger tomorrow? Next week?

originality.label: Originality
originality.desc: Does it bring something new, or is it a competent cover of a familiar tune?

presentation.label: Presentation
presentation.desc: How it looks on arrival. We eat with our eyes first.

concept.label: Concept
concept.desc: The idea behind it. Is there a point of view on the plate?

value.label: Value
value.desc: Quality and quantity for the yuan. Worth it, or are you paying for the postcode?

accuracy.label: Accuracy
accuracy.desc: Did it match the menu's promise? No mystery downgrades.

postBurger.label: Post-Burger
postBurger.desc: How you feel afterwards. Satisfied and happy, or full of grease and regret?

xFactor.label: X-Factor
xFactor.desc: The intangible. That special something a scorecard can never fully capture.

again.label: Order Again?
again.desc: Would the rater personally order this exact burger again?

recommend.label: Club Recommends?
recommend.desc: Would the rater send a friend here for this burger?

ranking.metric: Mean total across all club ratings of a burger, out of 80.
ranking.totalsAreComputed: A burger's total is always recomputed from its 20 sub-scores. Hand-written totals on old cards are ignored — the sum is the truth.
tiebreak: Higher mean on The Patty (the part you cannot fake).
Most recent rating wins, because momentum counts.

quorum.blurb: One palate is a rumour. A burger's score becomes an official Club Verdict once two or more members have filed a card. Until then it's Provisional — a solo scout report, taken on trust.

consensus.blurb: We score independently, then reveal. The gap between the table's highest and lowest card tells you how much we actually agreed — no averaging away a good argument.

awards.label: The Golden Bun
awards.desc: The highest score on the ledger.

awards.label: The Crave Cup
awards.desc: The one you can't stop thinking about.

awards.label: The Nori Prize
awards.desc: The most original idea on a bun.

awards.label: The Wooden Spatula
awards.desc: The cautionary tale. Lowest on record.

constitution.text: Fill your own card before anyone speaks. No table-talk until the pens are down.
A total is the sum of its 20 parts. Hand-written totals are heresy.
One palate is a rumour. Two or more makes it Ratified.
You are paying for the burger, not the neighbourhood.
Every score is public, forever. The ledger is the club.

constitution.clause: No Plate Too Big Or Small
constitution.text: Smash, pub, wagyu, or drunk-at-3am — all rise to be judged.
